evidence_quotes.quote: Video Games have been steadily growing in the last 15 or so years.
Many online games have seen the lights turned off never to return.
On April 29-2021 the game was shut down by Gamigo.

paragraphs.text: I write to the European Comission with sincerity in relation to the Digital Fairness Act. Video Games have been steadily growing in the last 15 or so years. Many online games have seen the lights turned off never to return. One such game until recently was Defiance released in 2013. Originally launched by Trion and later acquired by Gamigo, All the while now in the hands of Fawkes Games. Initially Defiance was buy to play. I paid the launch price of 59,99 for Defiance. On April 29-2021 the game was shut down by Gamigo. It was unplayable until recently even though I paid 59,99 price at launch. For a studio like Fawkes Games to relaunch a game like Defiance is nearly unheard of in the gaming industry. Most of these sorts of digital media/art are not preserved and pushed to the way side in favor of a new product. I write to the Digital Fairness Act and the European Comission not just for the fact that we as customers paid for the product but this digital media/art would otherwise be inaccessable. If we take a painting as an example, Someone paid for it so they own it and can look at it or use it any way they see fit. But the same cannot be said for a lot of Digital Media which is not preserved, Unlike Defiance a lot of Online games never see the light of day again. And inevitably with how the laws are currently written such media will eventually get lost again, The same goes for the relaunch of Defiance by Fawkes Games. Sincerely, A concerned gamer. Zappieroth/Remco Groentjes.
